summary: Israel struck Hamas military commander Izz al‑Din Haddad in Gaza, while Hezbollah drones probed Israel’s northern border and triggered alerts in the Galilee. Parallel diplomatic moves see the US and Israel preparing for possible action against Iran, with Trump poised to decide within 24 hours. Regional tensions rise as Lebanon’s southern border sees daily Israeli strikes and Iraqi leadership changes add a new dimension.
key_points: Israel eliminated Hamas senior commander Izz al‑Din Haddad in a targeted airstrike in Gaza.
Hezbollah launched multiple drones from Lebanon, hitting near‑misses in Kiryat Shmona and Metula.
US‑Israel officials warn of imminent military options against Iran, with Trump expected to decide within 24 hours.
Israel imposed a closed military zone along the Rosh HaNikra‑Akhziv coastal strip.
Iran faces severe gasoline shortages, reporting a daily deficit of at least 20 million L.
UAE accelerates a new oil pipeline to double export capacity via Fujairah by 2027.
Iraqi Prime Minister Ali al‑Zaidi was sworn in, consolidating command of the armed forces.

sections.executive_summary: Israel’s precision strike eliminated Hamas military chief Izz al‑Din Haddad, marking a significant degradation of Gaza’s command structure. Simultaneously, Hezbollah’s drone incursions into northern Israel triggered air‑defense alerts and underscored the volatile Lebanon front. Diplomatic channels indicate the United States, with Israeli backing, is on the brink of authorising a broader military option against Iran within the next 24 hours.

points: Trump is expected to decide within 24 hours on a possible military action against the Islamic Republic, with senior Israeli officials describing a “near‑term war” readiness (Source: @rodast_omiddana/71403).
German Chancellor Friedrich Merz reported a phone call with Trump confirming agreement that Iran must reopen the Strait of Hormuz and abandon nuclear ambitions (Source: @VahidOnline).
Iran faces a daily gasoline shortfall of at least 20 million liters, with production capped at 110‑115 million L against consumption of 130‑135 million L (Source: @VahidOnline/75487).
UAE announced acceleration of a new “West‑to‑East” pipeline to double oil export capacity via Fujairah by 2027, aiming to bypass the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @VahidOnline).

The Israeli Air Force carried out a high‑probability strike that killed Hamas commander Izz al‑Din Haddad after weeks of surveillance, marking the removal of the last senior Hamas military leader from the October 7 onslaught (Source: @manniefabian/58258).
IDF announced a closed military zone in the Rosh HaNikra‑Akhziv coastal strip, prohibiting civilian entry as of 21:20 on Thursday (Source: @yediotnews25/129330).

Hezbollah launched several drones from Lebanon; impacts were recorded in Kiryat Shmona and Metula, with two drones intercepted and others falling in open areas, causing no injuries (Source: @manniefabian/58251).

The IDF completed a rapid “Sulfur and Fire” drill on the Jordanian border, involving the 96th and 80th divisions, special forces, and air‑ground strikes, achieving 100 % reservist mobilization (Source: @manniefabian/58251).

Israeli officials reported successful interception of multiple hostile aircraft launched from Lebanon, with two downed and several crashes near Metula and Kiryat Shmona, confirming a breach of the cease‑fire (Source: @idf_telegram/23675).
Following the Haddad strike, Israeli forces continued operations in Gaza, targeting remaining Hamas leadership and infrastructure (Source: @manniefabian/58258).
PressTV reported a fresh wave of Zionist aggression in the West Bank, with settlers torching a mosque and storming the Al‑Aqsa compound in Jerusalem (Source: @presstv/190074).
Israeli military announced the closure of the Rosh HaNikra‑Akhziv coastal area, limiting movement for both civilians and militants (Source: @yediotnews25/129330).
Hezbollah’s drone activity prompted alerts in northern Israel, indirectly affecting security posture in the West Bank as forces are re‑allocated northward (Source: @idf_telegram/23675).

Hezbollah launched multiple drones from Lebanese territory toward northern Israel, with impacts near Kiryat Shmona and Metula; two drones were intercepted, others fell in open terrain (Source: @manniefabian/58251).
IDF reported additional drone incursions over Mount Meron, triggering sirens but causing no casualties (Source: @manniefabian/58260).
Israeli forces announced attacks on Hezbollah targets in several locations across southern Lebanon as retaliation (Source: @N12chat/207467).
PressTV noted continued escalation along Lebanon’s southern border, with near‑daily Israeli strikes prompting Hezbollah retaliation (Source: @PressTV).
Iraqi Prime Minister Ali al‑Zaidi was sworn in, assuming command of the armed forces, signaling a potential shift in Baghdad’s security posture (Source: @presstv/190061).
Reports from naya_foriraq indicated Israeli occupation forces conducting raids in Quneitra Governorate, southern Syria, targeting homes (Source: @naya_foriraq/75479).
Hezbollah’s cross‑border drone activity heightened tensions along the Israel‑Syria frontier, with IDF intercepting aircraft launched from Lebanon (Source: @idf_telegram/23675).
UAE announced acceleration of a new oil pipeline to double export capacity via Fujairah by 2027, enhancing its ability to bypass the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @VahidOnline).
German troop redeployment plans were halted, with the US pausing a 4,000‑troop deployment to Poland, reflecting shifting NATO focus toward the Middle East (Source: @asafroz/49229).
Saudi‑German diplomatic talks referenced opening the Strait of Hormuz and preventing Iran’s nuclear progress, aligning Gulf interests with US policy (Source: @VahidOnline).

key_developments: Israel’s airstrike eliminated Hamas senior commander Izz al‑Din Haddad in Gaza, removing the last senior military leader from the October 7 onslaught (Source: @manniefabian/58258).
Hezbollah launched multiple drones from Lebanon, striking near Kiryat Shmona and Metula; two were intercepted and others fell in open areas with no casualties (Source: @manniefabian/58251).
Trump is expected to decide within 24 hours on a possible military action against Iran, with Israeli officials indicating war readiness for days to weeks (Source: @rodast_omiddana/71403).
IDF imposed a closed military zone along the Rosh HaNikra‑Akhziv coastal strip, prohibiting civilian entry as of 21:20 Thursday (Source: @yediotnews25/129330).
PressTV reported intensified Israeli strikes on Lebanese towns and Hezbollah retaliation, marking a sharp escalation on the southern Lebanon front (Source: @PressTV).
UAE accelerated a new oil pipeline project to double export capacity via Fujairah by 2027, aiming to bypass the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @VahidOnline).
Iraqi Prime Minister Ali al‑Zaidi was sworn in, consolidating command of the armed forces and potentially reshaping Iraq’s regional security role (Source: @presstv/190061).
sections.threat_assessment: The elimination of Izz al‑Din Haddad degrades Hamas’s operational command but may provoke retaliatory attacks from remaining militant cells. Hezbollah’s drone incursions demonstrate a growing capability to challenge Israeli air defenses, raising the risk of broader escalation on the Lebanon front. Simultaneously, US‑Israel coordination on a possible strike against Iran, coupled with Iranian fuel shortages, creates a volatile environment where a miscalculation could trigger a multi‑theater conflict.
sections.regional_response: Gulf states, notably the UAE, are bolstering strategic infrastructure to mitigate potential Strait of Hormuz closures, while Germany’s pause on troop deployments to Poland reflects a reallocation of NATO resources toward the Middle East. Iraq’s new leadership may seek to balance internal stability with external pressures, potentially influencing Shiite militia dynamics in Syria and Lebanon.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Iranian decision‑making is critical as Trump’s pending verdict could reshape US engagement in the region. Hezbollah’s drone tactics suggest an evolving asymmetric threat that may be supported by Iranian Quds Force assets. Israeli closed‑zone enforcement along the northern coast indicates preparation for possible ground operations or expanded air‑defense coverage. Continued surveillance of fuel logistics in Iran will inform assessments of domestic unrest and its impact on regional proxy activities.
